phases.title: Core Identity & Strategic Context
phases.description: Understand who the organization is and what drives it.

questions.text: What is your organization's primary industry, approximate size (revenue and/or employees), and core business model in 1–2 sentences?
questions.why: This helps align AI opportunities to your actual business and scale.

questions.text: What are your top 3–5 strategic priorities or goals for the next 3–5 years? (e.g., revenue growth, cost leadership, customer retention, sustainability, geographic expansion)
questions.why: AI use cases will be prioritized based on how well they support these goals.

questions.text: Who are your 2–3 main competitors, and what do you see as your current key differentiators (what makes you win or lose deals)?
questions.why: Helps identify where AI can create or defend competitive advantage.

phases.title: Current Performance & Pain Points
phases.description: Identify friction in today's operations (strong input for Horizon 1).

questions.text: What are the biggest operational or process challenges right now (high costs, quality issues, delays, compliance risks, talent shortages, etc.)?
questions.why: These are prime candidates for quick AI-driven improvements.

questions.text: Which functions or processes have the highest manual/repetitive effort, error rates, or employee frustration? Please name 2–4 examples.
questions.why: High-volume, rules-based work is usually easiest to augment with AI.

questions.text: Which 2–3 key performance metrics are most under pressure or hardest to move (e.g., customer churn, gross margin, order cycle time, forecast accuracy)?
questions.why: We can look for AI levers that directly influence these KPIs.

questions.text: How would you describe your current AI maturity? (Choose or describe: No usage → Experimenting with pilots → Active in 1–2 functions → Scaling enterprise-wide → AI is core to strategy)
questions.why: This determines realistic speed and scope of recommendations.

questions.text: Do you have a centralized data platform, data warehouse/lake, or strong data governance practices? How would you rate data readiness for AI (low / medium / high)?
questions.why: Data quality & accessibility is often the #1 blocker.

questions.text: What AI-related talent, teams or external partners do you currently have or work with? (data scientists, ML engineers, consultants, cloud/AI providers, etc.)
questions.why: Helps gauge build vs. buy decisions.

phases.title: Ambition, Risk Appetite & Long-term View
phases.description: Shape Horizons 2 and 3 — understand boldness and boundaries.

questions.text: How aggressive is your appetite for AI-driven transformation? (Mostly quick efficiency wins / Also want new AI-enhanced products/services / Aiming to disrupt the industry or invent new business models)
questions.why: This guides how much emphasis we put on H2 and H3 vs. H1.

questions.text: What are your biggest concerns or non-negotiables regarding AI adoption? (ethics/responsible AI, regulation, job impact, data security/privacy, explainability, vendor lock-in, etc.)
questions.why: We will filter and flag ideas accordingly.

questions.text: Looking 7–10+ years ahead: what would 'winning' look like in your industry if AI continues to advance rapidly? Any big threats or once-in-a-generation opportunities you see?
questions.why: This unlocks truly transformative Horizon 3 thinking.

optional_deep_dive_triggers.condition: customer experience OR churn mentioned in priorities
optional_deep_dive_triggers.question: Can you describe a recent customer pain point or frustrating journey step (for customers or employees)?

optional_deep_dive_triggers.condition: cost OR margin OR efficiency in priorities
optional_deep_dive_triggers.question: Which cost categories or processes are growing fastest or are hardest to control right now?
